data.body: STORE ASSESSMENT (per user direction): RECENT (signing-live) unsigned in-scope event types: STATUS(9), EDIT(6) [txn.py transitions/claims], FILE_IMPACT(4) [set-file-impact], TICKET_DIGEST(6), DONE_ENRICH(6)/ENQUEUE_ENRICH [enrich drain], SIGNATURE(2)/COMPLETION_VERDICT(1) [attestations], REVIEW_RESULT(4) [code_review = already exempt ticket type]. So MULTIPLE write paths bypass the signing seam, not just txn.py. Design question: route AUTHORED writes (STATUS/EDIT/FILE_IMPACT/...) through ONE shared finalize+sign seam (per user: no copy-paste); decide policy for SYSTEM/DERIVED events (TICKET_DIGEST/enrich/SIGNATURE/COMPLETION_VERDICT) = sign-if-keyed OR gate-exempt by event type (they are derived/attestation, not authored work). Architecture-map subagent running to produce the shared-seam design + full call-site inventory.
